message:
fix(interactive): handle agent-busy state gracefully in tree navigation and branch picker

Tree navigation (tree_ui.rs):
- Change start_tree_navigation to return bool indicating success,
  allowing callers to detect when the agent is locked and restore the
  previous UI state instead of silently dropping the user's context
- When start_tree_navigation returns false (agent busy), restore the
  TreeUiState to Selector, SummaryPrompt, or CustomPrompt so the user
  can retry without losing their position in the tree UI flow
- Clone pending/instructions before moving into start_tree_navigation
  so the originals survive a failed attempt and remain available for
  the UI state restoration

Interactive module (interactive.rs):
- Add agent_handle() accessor to expose the Agent Arc<Mutex<>> for
  external lock acquisition (used by tests to simulate agent-busy)
- Replace downcast_ref/downcast pair with a single downcast match to
  eliminate redundant type check on PiMsg handling, following the
  pattern of try-downcast-then-fallback

Tests (tui_state.rs):
- Add test verifying the summary prompt stays open when the agent is
  busy during tree branch selection, confirming the UI state is
  correctly restored
- Add test verifying the branch picker stays open when the agent is
  busy during branch switch, with proper status message display

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
